title: CEF Transport: €1.1 billion available for infrastructure projects across the EU
content: The Commission launched the 2026 call for proposals under the Connecting Europe Facility (CEF), making €1.1 billion available to build and modernise transport infrastructure on the EU's trans-European transport network (TEN-T), as well as in Ukraine and Moldova.
snippet: The Commission launched the 2026 call for proposals under the Connecting Europe Facility (CEF), making €1.1 billion available to build and modernise transport infrastructure on the EU's trans-European

title: Better Homes Partnerships: Call for expressions of interest launched
content: The European Commission has launched a call for expressions of interest under the Better Homes Partnerships initiative to identify concrete projects, partnerships and replicable solutions that can help accelerate affordable, high-quality home renovations across the EU.
snippet: The European Commission has launched a call for expressions of interest under the Better Homes Partnerships initiative to identify concrete projects, partnerships and replicable solutions that can

title: Take a splash: European bathing waters remain clean
content: The latest EU bathing water report finds majority of coastal waters and inland bathing areas are of ‘excellent’ quality. Find out more.
snippet: The latest EU bathing water report finds majority of coastal waters and inland bathing areas are of ‘excellent’ quality. Find out more.

title: Inaccurate clothing labels a systemic issue, EU testing finds
content: Testing of 132 clothing items during an EU campaign found that their labels did not correctly reflect the materials used in 49 samples (37%).
snippet: Testing of 132 clothing items during an EU campaign found that their labels did not correctly reflect the materials used in 49 samples (37%).

title: European Union and South Africa strengthen research and innovation cooperation at 18th JSTCC meeting
content: 30 years after the signing of their bilateral agreement, the EU and South Africa reaffirmed their commitment to further strengthen their R&I partnership.
snippet: 30 years after the signing of their bilateral agreement, the EU and South Africa reaffirmed their commitment to further strengthen their R&I partnership.

title: Electronic products tested for hazardous substances
content: EU-funded testing campaign for levels of hazardous substances in 173 electric and electronic gadgets found that half exceeded the permitted limits.
snippet: EU-funded testing campaign for levels of hazardous substances in 173 electric and electronic gadgets found that half exceeded the permitted limits.

title: Radio-controlled toys tested for signal interference
content: EU-funded testing of 88 radio-controlled toys for 3-14-year-olds found that 53 (60 %) failed to meet EU standards for interference.
snippet: EU-funded testing of 88 radio-controlled toys for 3-14-year-olds found that 53 (60 %) failed to meet EU standards for interference.

title: Commission renews mandate of expert group on the competitiveness of the rail supply industry
content: The European Commission has renewed the mandate of the Expert Group on the Competitiveness of the Rail Supply Industry (RSI) until 31 December 2028 and launched a call for applications to select new stakeholder members.
snippet: The European Commission has renewed the mandate of the Expert Group on the Competitiveness of the Rail Supply Industry (RSI) until 31 December 2028 and launched a call for applications to select new

title: The eight host organisations for the 5th EUDIS Defence Hackathon are officially confirmed
content: Eight organisations across Europe have been selected to host the Autumn 2026 edition of the EUDIS Defence Hackathon, taking place simultaneously across eight locations on 15–17 October 2026.
snippet: Eight organisations across Europe have been selected to host the Autumn 2026 edition of the EUDIS Defence Hackathon, taking place simultaneously across eight locations on 15–17 October 2026.

title: Protect your rights across EU borders with new social security rules
content: The EU has revised its social security coordination rules. Read on to find out how this affects you as a worker or an employer.
snippet: The EU has revised its social security coordination rules. Read on to find out how this affects you as a worker or an employer.

title: Biomethane Mechanism: Connecting buyers and sellers
content: The European Commission has officially launched today the Biomethane Mechanism, a key initiative under the EU Energy and Raw Materials Platform. Designed to accelerate the growth of Europe’s biomethane market, the mechanism is a voluntary, free of charge and innovative tool.
snippet: The European Commission has officially launched today the Biomethane Mechanism, a key initiative under the EU Energy and Raw Materials Platform. Designed to accelerate the growth of Europe’s

title: EU-funded technology strengthening Europe’s security
content: On 17-18 June, the Commission showcased how innovative EU-funded European technologies will help European law enforcement authorities to fight cross-border organised crime and terrorism with faster, more accurate and interoperable identification and detection technology.
snippet: On 17-18 June, the Commission showcased how innovative EU-funded European technologies will help European law enforcement authorities to fight cross-border organised crime and terrorism with faster,

title: Study on consumer understanding of the EU tyre label applied since 1 May 2021 - Publications Office of the EU
content: This study, commissioned by the European Commission, examines how the revised EU tyre label ......
snippet: This study, commissioned by the European Commission, examines how the revised EU tyre label ......

title: Study on consumer understanding of the EU energy labels applied since 2021 - Publications Office of the EU
content: This study evaluates two pillars of the EU Energy Labelling Regulation (EU) 2017/1369: the rescaled A–G energy labels introduced in March 2021 for six product groups, and the European Product Registry for Energy Labelling (EPREL).
snippet: This study evaluates two pillars of the EU Energy Labelling Regulation (EU) 2017/1369: the rescaled A–G energy labels introduced in March 2021 for six product groups, and the European Product Registry

title: Commission welcomes Council agreement paving the way for stronger EU military mobility
content: The Commission welcomes the Council’s general approach on its negotiating mandate for the Military Mobility Regulation.
snippet: The Commission welcomes the Council’s general approach on its negotiating mandate for the Military Mobility Regulation.

title: High-Level roundtable on water and circular economy brings together key European stakeholders
content: The European Commission convened a high-level roundtable on “Water and Circular Economy”, gathering leading representatives from industry, utilities, research institutions, civil society, and EU-level organisations to advance the development of a water-smart and resource-efficient economy
snippet: The European Commission convened a high-level roundtable on “Water and Circular Economy”, gathering leading representatives from industry, utilities, research institutions, civil society, and EU-level

title: EEA and Eionet welcome Ukraine as a cooperating country
content: The European Environment Agency (EEA) and its European Environment Information and Observation Network (Eionet) welcomed Ukraine as a cooperating country, extending the EEA's country network to 32 member and 8 cooperating countries across Europe.
snippet: The European Environment Agency (EEA) and its European Environment Information and Observation Network (Eionet) welcomed Ukraine as a cooperating country, extending the EEA's country network to 32

title: Interview: Making affordable housing for young Europeans
content: Why has housing become a top concern for European citizens, especially young people, and what can be done at the EU level? Deputy Director-General Normunds Popens from DG EAC discuss these questions with the head of the European Commission’s Housing Task Force Matthew Baldwin.
snippet: Why has housing become a top concern for European citizens, especially young people, and what can be done at the EU level? Deputy Director-General Normunds Popens from DG EAC discuss these questions

title: Operation ZAFIRO: the Largest Coordinated General Aviation Operation to Date
content: Operation ZAFIRO is the third international coordinated operation under the COLIBRI Project, a partnership between the European Union (EU) and the World Customs Organization (WCO).
snippet: Operation ZAFIRO is the third international coordinated operation under the COLIBRI Project, a partnership between the European Union (EU) and the World Customs Organization (WCO).

title: President von der Leyen participates in G7 Summit in Evian
content: Leaders discussed imbalances and overcapacities in global economy, as well as conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine. They also addressed AI challenges and the protection of children online.
snippet: Leaders discussed imbalances and overcapacities in global economy, as well as conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine. They also addressed AI challenges and the protection of children online.

title: Commission approves €85 million Irish State aid for agricultural companies facing increased fuel prices
content: The European Commission has approved an €85 million Irish State aid scheme to support agricultural companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis.
snippet: The European Commission has approved an €85 million Irish State aid scheme to support agricultural companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis.

title: Commission approves €1 billion Slovak State aid scheme to support cleantech manufacturing capacity
content: The European Commission has approved a €1 billion Slovak scheme to support clean technology (cleantech) manufacturing capacity in line with the objectives of the Clean Industrial Deal.
snippet: The European Commission has approved a €1 billion Slovak scheme to support clean technology (cleantech) manufacturing capacity in line with the objectives of the Clean Industrial Deal.

title: Commission strengthens migration management along the Channel route
content: On 16 June, the Commission presented an EU action plan for the Channel migratory route, setting out a comprehensive response to the challenges posed by illegal migration along this route, focusing on on three priority areas to be tackled with support from EU agencies and in cooperation with the UK.

title: Joint Statement by the Minister of Investment of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan and the European Commissioner for the Mediterranean
content: The Government of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan and the European Union are pleased to announce that the Jordan-EU Investment Conference 2026, will be held under the patronage of His Majesty King Abdullah II ibn Al Hussein and with the participation of Her Excellency Ursula von der Leyen...

title: EU-South Africa mark 30 years of research and innovation cooperation
content: The EU and South Africa celebrated 30 years of bilateral research and innovation cooperation and focused on the evolution of this key partnership.
snippet: The EU and South Africa celebrated 30 years of bilateral research and innovation cooperation and focused on the evolution of this key partnership.

title: Second meeting of the Talent for Innovation Attraction Platform
content: Experts from EU countries working on migration, education and research, representatives from universities, national research centres, and other stakeholders exchanged on the attraction and retention of international students and researchers.
snippet: Experts from EU countries working on migration, education and research, representatives from universities, national research centres, and other stakeholders exchanged on the attraction and retention

title: Commissioner Šuica discusses the Pact for the Mediterranean with the youth
content: Tomorrow, Commissioner for the Mediterranean Dubravka Šuica, together with Marilena Raouna, Cyprus' Deputy Minister for European Affairs, will engage in a discussion with 30 young leaders from the Southern Mediterranean.

title: State of the Digital Decade report: progress made, but gaps remain
content: The report assesses how the EU has made progress on its 2030 digital transformation targets, and provides recommendations on how to close the remaining gaps.
snippet: The report assesses how the EU has made progress on its 2030 digital transformation targets, and provides recommendations on how to close the remaining gaps.

title: First EDIP Call closes and receives high number of proposals
content: 83 proposals were received by the European Commission for the very first call for proposals of the European Defence Industry Programme’s (EDIP) that closed yesterday at 17:00h.
snippet: 83 proposals were received by the European Commission for the very first call for proposals of the European Defence Industry Programme’s (EDIP) that closed yesterday at 17:00h.
